## UNIT: Plan Slice S03 ("Home + Services Pages") — Milestone M001

All relevant context has been preloaded below — start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [x] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [x] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [ ] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [ ] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [ ] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [ ] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

---

### Slice Research
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S03/S03-RESEARCH.md`

# S03: Home + Services Pages — Research

**Date:** 2026-03-25

## Summary

Both `src/pages/Home.jsx` and `src/pages/Services.jsx` are fully implemented — they were built ahead of schedule during earlier task work. The pages are feature-complete: Home has hero, stats strip, bento services grid, "Why Oriflect" section, ROI CTA, and closing CTA. Services has a hero, three alternating `ServiceSection` components with deliverables/metrics/modules, a quote form with success state, and a closing CTA.

The build is clean (`npm run build` → 34 modules, 0 errors, 308 kB JS bundle). Routes are registered in `App.jsx` under the shared `Layout` wrapper. The `ghost-grid` CSS class referenced in Services hero exists in `index.css`. All Tailwind design tokens (`surface-container`, `primary`, `on-surface-variant`, etc.) resolve correctly.

The slice's primary work — converting mockup content into React components with correct design tokens and working nav — is already done. S03 execution should be verification-only: confirm pages render visually, nav active state works, and no regressions exist.

## Recommendation

Run a visual check against the dev server. Verify: (1) Home and Services render at localhost:5173, (2) nav highlights the correct active item on each page, (3) the Services quote form shows success state on submit. If all three pass, mark S03 complete and move directly to S04 planning.

No new implementation needed. Do not re-implement content that already exists.

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Problem | Existing Solution | Why Use It |
|---------|------------------|------------|
| Services quote form state | `useState` in Services.jsx — already implemented | It's done; `submitted` state toggles the success panel |
| Active nav highlighting | `NavLink` with `isActive` in Nav.jsx | Already using React Router's built-in active detection |

## Existing Code and Patterns

- `src/pages/Home.jsx` — Full hero + stats + services bento + Why Oriflect + ROI CTA + closing CTA. All sections complete. Uses `Link` from react-router-dom.
- `src/pages/Services.jsx` — Three `ServiceSection` components rendered from data array (alternating layout via idx parity). Quote form with `formData` state + `submitted` success panel. Uses `Link` to /contact and /roi.
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — Wraps `<Nav />` + `<Outlet />` + `<Footer />`. All pages inherit this automatically.
- `src/App.jsx` — Routes `/` → Home, `/services` → Services already registered.
- `src/index.css` — Contains `grid-bg`, `ghost-grid`, `glass-header`, `hero-gradient` utility classes referenced by both pages.

## Constraints

- `pt-[72px]` is hardcoded on both page root divs to clear the fixed nav — this must be preserved.
- `ghost-grid` class exists in index.css; if it's removed or renamed, the Services hero breaks silently (no error, just missing background).
- Images use `lh3.googleusercontent.com` URLs (one in Home hero) — per D006, acceptable for launch; may 403 in some environments.
- `material-symbols-outlined` icons depend on the Google Fonts link in `index.html` — if that's absent, icons fall back to raw text strings.

## Common Pitfalls

- **Re-implementing existing components** — Both pages exist and are correct. Do not overwrite them.
- **Missing `ghost-grid` class** — Not a Tailwind utility; it's defined in `src/index.css`. If the CSS is audited and this class is removed thinking it's unused, Services hero breaks.
- **Services form `select` styling** — Uses `appearance-none` + `outline-none` with no custom chevron. Looks intentional (minimal aesthetic), not a bug.

## Open Risks

- Visual regression against original HTML mockups has not been formally verified — mockup sections may have content the React version omitted. Low risk; pages were authored to match mockups during S01/S02 period.
- `lh3.googleusercontent.com` hero image in Home may 403 in CI/production. No fallback is currently implemented. Noted in D006 as user's responsibility.

## Skills Discovered

| Technology | Skill | Status |
|------------|-------|--------|
| React + Tailwind UI | frontend-design | installed (available in system) |

## Sources

- Direct file inspection: `src/pages/Home.jsx`, `src/pages/Services.jsx`, `src/App.jsx`, `src/components/Layout.jsx`
- Build verification: `npm run build` — clean, 0 errors

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |
| D007 | S01 | verification | S01 verification strategy | Build + grep assertions (no test framework) | S01 is a scaffold slice — contract proof via `npm run build` + token grep is sufficient; no UI logic to unit test | Yes — introduce Playwright in S02 or S05 if visual regression needed |
| D008 | S01 | convention | Project subdirectory | Vite project at `oriflect/` not workspace root | Scaffold was created pre-GSD; deviation is acceptable; all S02+ work targets `oriflect/src/` | No |

# Requirements

Then:
0. If `REQUIREMENTS.md` was preloaded above, identify which Active requirements the roadmap says this slice owns or supports. These are the requirements this plan must deliver — every owned requirement needs at least one task that directly advances it, and verification must prove the requirement is met.
1. Read the templates:
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/plan.md`
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/task-plan.md`
2. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during planning, without overriding required plan formatting
3. Define slice-level verification first — the objective stopping condition for this slice:
   - For non-trivial slices: plan actual test files with real assertions. Name the files. The first task creates them (initially failing). Remaining tasks make them pass.
   - For simple slices: executable commands or script assertions are fine.
   - If the project is non-trivial and has no test framework, the first task should set one up.
   - If this slice establishes a boundary contract, verification must exercise that contract.
4. Plan observability and diagnostics explicitly:
   - For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices, include an `Observability / Diagnostics` section in the slice plan.
   - Define how a future agent will inspect state, detect failure, and localize the problem.
   - Prefer structured logs/events, stable error codes/types, status surfaces, and persisted failure state over ad hoc debug text.
   - Include at least one verification check for a diagnostic or failure-path signal when relevant.
5. Fill the `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections truthfully:
   - State whether the slice proves contract, integration, operational, or final-assembly behavior.
   - Say whether real runtime or human/UAT is required.
   - Name the wiring introduced in this slice and what still remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end.
6. Decompose the slice into tasks, each fitting one context window
7. Every task in the slice plan should be written as an executable increment with:
   - a concrete, action-oriented title
   - the inline task entry fields defined in the plan.md template (Why / Files / Do / Verify / Done when)
   - a matching task plan containing description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
8. Each task needs: title, description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
9. If verification includes test files, ensure the first task includes creating them with expected assertions (they should fail initially — that's correct)
10. Write `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S03/S03-PLAN.md`
11. Write individual task plans in `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S03/tasks/`: `T01-PLAN.md`, `T02-PLAN.md`, etc.
12. **Self-audit the plan before continuing.** Walk through each check — if any fail, fix the plan files before moving on:
    - **Completion semantics:** If every task were completed exactly as written, the slice goal/demo should actually be true at the claimed proof level. Do not allow a task plan that only scaffolds toward a future working state.
    - **Requirement coverage:** Every must-have in the slice maps to at least one task. No must-have is orphaned.
    - **Task completeness:** Every task has steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output — none are blank or vague.
    - **Dependency correctness:** Task ordering is consistent. No task references work from a later task.
    - **Key links planned:** For every pair of artifacts that must connect (component → API, API → database, form → handler), there is an explicit step that wires them — not just "create X" and "create Y" in separate tasks with no connection step.
    - **Scope sanity:** Target 2–5 steps and 3–8 files per task. 6–8 steps or 8–10 files is a warning — consider splitting. 10+ steps or 12+ files — must split. Each task must be completable in a single fresh context window.
    - **Context compliance:** If context/research artifacts or `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` exist, the plan honors locked decisions and doesn't include deferred or out-of-scope items.
    - **Requirement coverage:** If `REQUIREMENTS.md` exists, every Active requirement this slice owns (per the roadmap) maps to at least one task with verification that proves the requirement is met. No owned requirement is left without a task. No task claims to satisfy a requirement that is Deferred or Out of Scope.
    - **Proof honesty:** The `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections match what this slice will actually prove, and they do not imply live end-to-end completion if only fixture or contract proof is planned.
    - **Feature completeness:** Every task produces real, user-facing progress — not just internal scaffolding. If the slice has a UI surface, at least one task builds the real UI (not a placeholder). If the slice has an API, at least one task connects it to a real data source (not hardcoded returns). If every task were completed and you showed the result to a non-technical stakeholder, they should see real product progress, not developer artifacts.
13. If planning produced structural decisions (e.g. verification strategy, observability strategy, technology choices, patterns to follow), append them to `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`
14. Commit: `docs(S03): add slice plan`
15. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

The slice directory and tasks/ subdirectory already exist. Do NOT mkdir. You are on the slice branch; all work stays here.

**You MUST write the file `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S03\S03-PLAN.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Slice S03 planned."

The risk is not implementation but regression-checking: nav active state, design tokens, Services form success path. No test framework exists and the project is a UI slice, so verification is browser-based against the running dev server.

arguments.content:
# S03: Home + Services Pages

**Goal:** Confirm Home and Services pages are fully rendered, navigable via React Router, and pass visual verification against the Axiom Navy design system.
**Demo:** Navigate to `localhost:5173/` and `/services` — both pages render with correct design tokens, nav highlights the active page, and the Services quote form shows a success panel on submit.

## Must-Haves

- Home page renders at `/` with hero, stats strip, bento services grid, "Why Oriflect" section, ROI CTA, and closing CTA
- Services page renders at `/services` with three `ServiceSection` components, quote form, and closing CTA
- Nav active state highlights `/` on Home and `/services` on Services
- Services quote form shows success panel on submit (no page reload)
- `npm run build` remains clean (0 errors) after S03

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: integration
- Real runtime required: yes (dev server)
- Human/UAT required: yes (visual design check)

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` — exits 0 with no errors
- Dev server running; navigate to `http://localhost:5173/` — Home renders with all sections
- Navigate to `http://localhost:5173/services` — Services renders with three ServiceSection blocks
- Nav active item is highlighted on each page (gold underline/color via NavLink `isActive`)
- Fill and submit the Services quote form — success panel appears without page reload
- Browser console shows no unhandled JS errors on either page

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: browser console errors surface broken imports or missing CSS classes
- Inspection surfaces: `npm run build` output for bundle errors; browser DevTools Network tab for 4xx on assets
- Failure visibility: React error boundary (if triggered) renders red overlay; missing Tailwind class is visually obvious (unstyled element)
- Redaction constraints: none

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `src/components/Layout.jsx`, `src/components/Nav.jsx`, `src/components/Footer.jsx`, React Router context, Tailwind tokens from `tailwind.config.js`, CSS classes in `src/index.css`
- New wiring introduced in this slice: none — pages and routes already exist
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: S04 (About, Blog, Contact), S05 (mobile polish), S06 (ROI calculator, client portal)

## Tasks

- [ ] **T01: Visual verification of Home and Services pages** `est:30m`
  - Why: Research confirmed both pages are implemented; this task is the objective proof that they meet S03 acceptance criteria in a real browser session
  - Files: `oriflect/src/pages/Home.jsx`, `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`, `oriflect/src/index.css`
  - Do: Start `npm run dev` in `oriflect/`. Open `localhost:5173/`. Verify each Home section renders. Navigate to `/services`. Verify all three ServiceSection components render. Check nav active state on both pages. Submit the Services quote form with test data. Verify success panel appears. Check browser console for errors. Run `npm run build` to confirm clean bundle.
  - Verify: All checklist items in the Verification section above pass; browser console clean; build exits 0
  - Done when: All six verification checks pass with no regressions

## Files Likely Touched

- `oriflect/src/pages/Home.jsx` — read-only verification
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — read-only verification (minor fix only if a bug is found)
- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — read-only verification
- `oriflect/src/index.css` — read-only verification (confirm `ghost-grid` class present)

---
estimated_steps: 6
estimated_files: 4
---

# T01: Visual verification of Home and Services pages

**Slice:** S03 — Home + Services Pages
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

Both pages were implemented ahead of schedule. This task runs the dev server and performs the objective verification that proves S03 is complete: all sections render, nav active state is correct, the Services quote form shows a success panel, and the build remains clean. Fix any minor regressions found during verification — but do not re-implement content that already exists.

## Steps

1. `cd oriflect && npm run dev` — start dev server, confirm it binds to localhost:5173
2. Open browser at `http://localhost:5173/` — walk through each Home section (hero, stats strip, bento services grid, "Why Oriflect", ROI CTA, closing CTA) and confirm they render with Axiom Navy design tokens (dark surfaces, muted gold, Space Grotesk font, no rounded corners)
3. Confirm nav shows Home as active (gold underline or text color change on the Home link)
4. Navigate to `http://localhost:5173/services` — confirm three ServiceSection blocks render with alternating layout, plus closing CTA; confirm nav shows Services as active
5. Fill in the Services quote form (Name, Company, Email, Service select, Message) and click Submit — confirm success panel appears without page reload and browser console shows no errors
6. Run `npm run build` — confirm exit 0, 0 errors; note bundle size for reference

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Home page renders all sections with correct design tokens (no plain white/unstyled sections)
- [ ] Services page renders three ServiceSection components with alternating layout
- [ ] Nav `NavLink` active state visible on Home at `/` and on Services at `/services`
- [ ] Services quote form success panel appears on submit (client-side, no reload)
- [ ] Browser console has no unhandled JS errors on either page
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` exits with code 0 and prints no errors
- Browser at `localhost:5173/` shows hero image/text, stats strip, grid of service cards, "Why Oriflect" section, and two CTA banners
- Browser at `localhost:5173/services` shows three alternating service sections, quote form, and closing CTA
- Nav active link is visually distinct (gold/highlighted) on each respective page
- Submitting the quote form renders a success panel (not a new page)
- DevTools console: no red errors on either page

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: None — this task is verification only
- How a future agent inspects this: `npm run build` output; browser DevTools console for runtime errors; visual scan of rendered pages
- Failure state exposed: If a section is missing or unstyled, the failure is visually obvious; console errors surface broken imports; build errors surface missing modules

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/pages/Home.jsx` — existing implementation to verify
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — existing implementation to verify
- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — active-state NavLink logic to verify
- `oriflect/src/index.css` — must contain `ghost-grid`, `hero-gradient`, `glass-header` classes
- `oriflect/src/App.jsx` — routes `/` and `/services` must be registered under Layout
- S02 summary: Nav + Footer + Layout components are in place

## Expected Output

- All verification checks pass — no new files written unless a minor regression is found and patched
- If a bug is found: targeted fix to the minimal file affected, with a note in the task summary
- Clean `npm run build` confirming no regressions introduced by S02 or earlier work

Slice S03 planned.

**Decomposition reasoning:** Research confirmed both pages are already implemented and the build is clean. This makes S03 a verification-only slice — the risk isn't building the right thing, it's proving that what was built actually works end-to-end in a real browser. One task is the right shape: start the dev server, walk both pages visually, exercise the Services form success path, confirm nav active state, run the build. Splitting this across multiple tasks would create false seams with no real boundary.

**Verification strategy:** Integration-level proof against a real dev server. No test framework introduced here (D007 — build + runtime assertion is sufficient for UI pages; Playwright deferred to S05 if visual regression becomes a concern). The stopping condition is explicit: six named checks that must all pass.

**Plan:** `S03-PLAN.md` + `T01-PLAN.md` written, committed to `gsd/M001/S03`.
